2606.21579.title: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of VLMs for Zero-shot Procedural Mistake Detection
2606.21579.paper_summary: Procedural mistake detection is important for quality control and user assistance across many disciplines. Recent work in this field has achieved significant gains by using the reasoning capabilities of Video-Language Models (VLMs) as components within multi-stage pipelines, which consist of separate modules for supervised temporal action segmentation, error detection, and explainability. Consequently, they remain dependent on tailored training datasets and require task-specific training, limiting their wider applicability. To remedy this, we introduce zero-shot procedural mistake detection and propose a unified Zero-shot Procedural Mistake detection (ZeProM) framework that jointly solves procedural mistake detection and temporal action segmentation with a single pre-trained VLM. By evaluating our framework on two canonical mistake detection benchmarks, EgoPER and CaptainCook4D, we find that ZeProM can perform these tasks successfully, while approaching, or even outperforming, the performance of fully supervised methods. For instance, we achieve a 4.4 point improvement in EDA and a 2.0 point improvement in F1@.5 on average over all five EgoPER tasks compared to the strongest supervised methods. Overall, our results show the potential of unified methods for procedural mistake detection, and we hope this will steer the field away from highly complex pipelines and toward more generally applicable solutions.
2606.21579.paper_summary_zh: 程序錯誤檢測對於許多學科的質量控制和用戶輔助至關重要。<lb/>該領域的近期研究通過將視頻語言模型（VLMs）的推理能力作為多階段管道中的組件，取得了顯著的進展，這些管道由監督的時間行動分割、錯誤檢測和可解釋性等單獨模塊組成。<lb/>因此，它們仍然依賴於量身定制的訓練數據集，並需要特定任務的訓練，限制了其更廣泛的適用性。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們引入了零樣本程序錯誤檢測，並提出了一個統一的零樣本程序錯誤檢測（ZeProM）框架，該框架使用單個預訓練的VLM共同解決程序錯誤檢測和時間行動分割。<lb/>通過在兩個經典的錯誤檢測基準EgoPER和CaptainCook4D上評估我們的框架，我們發現ZeProM能夠成功執行這些任務，並接近或甚至超越完全監督方法的性能。<lb/>例如，與最強的監督方法相比，我們在所有五個EgoPER任務中平均實現了4.4點的EDA改進和2.0點的F1@.5改進。<lb/>總體而言，我們的結果顯示了統一方法在程序錯誤檢測中的潛力，我們希望這能引導該領域擺脫高度複雜的管道，朝向更具普遍適用性的解決方案。
2606.21579.author: Serdar Ozsoy et.al.
2606.21579.authors: Serdar Ozsoy, Lars Doorenbos, Federico Spurio, Gianpiero Francesca, Juergen Gall
